name: Amazon Connect CCaaS API
what_it_does: Amazon Connect API for cloud contact center as a service (CCaaS). Enables AI agents to query contact records, manage agent states, retrieve real-time and historical analytics, configure IVR flows, and integrate with Amazon's AI services (Lex, Polly, Transcribe) for AI-powered contact center automation.
use_cases: Querying contact center metrics and agent performance from CX analytics agents
Retrieving call recordings and transcripts from customer experience automation agents
Managing Amazon Connect routing profiles and queues from contact center admin agents
Building AI-powered IVR and chatbot integration via Amazon Lex from CX automation agents
not_for: Organizations not on AWS (Amazon Connect is AWS-native)
Simple phone systems without contact center complexity
Real-time voice processing without proper AWS Connect setup
best_when: An enterprise using Amazon Connect for their contact center wants AI agents to analyze performance, automate routing, and integrate CX workflows with AWS services.
avoid_when: You need pre-built contact center features with minimal setup — Amazon Connect requires significant AWS configuration. PRIVACY: Call recordings and customer interaction data are highly sensitive.

auth.notes: AWS IAM authentication. Service account with connect:* permissions needed. Use IAM roles with least privilege. Never use root credentials.

pricing.free_tier_limits: AWS Free Tier: 90 minutes/month of usage for 12 months
pricing.paid_tiers: Per minute pricing: ~$0.018/minute for inbound; additional charges for add-ons
pricing.notes: Usage-based pricing per minute plus feature add-ons. Cost-effective for mid-to-large contact centers.

performance.rate_limits: Amazon Connect API: rate limits per API action, documented in AWS docs

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Call recordings and customer interaction transcripts are highly sensitive
No public MCP server — requires AWS SDK integration
Amazon Connect requires significant AWS configuration before API use
HIPAA and PCI compliance requires specific AWS Connect configurations
Real-time metrics API is separate from historical metrics — both needed for full observability
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Customer call recordings and interaction data. HIPAA, PCI-DSS, FedRAMP, SOC1/2. AWS IAM with least privilege. High compliance posture required for healthcare/financial.

security_score_components.security_notes: Contact center with sensitive customer call data. HIPAA, PCI-DSS, FedRAMP, SOC1/2. AWS IAM. Data residency by region. Enterprise compliance posture.
